authorities.name: U.S. Geological Survey (Geographic Names Information System)

sources.title: First Creek — Nevada Department of Wildlife (Washoe County, Western Region, "Creek or Stream")
sources.quotedText: County: Washoe. Region: Western. Type of Water: Creek or Stream.

sources.title: Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners, CR 25-16, Fishing Seasons and Regulations (Jan. 1, 2026 – Dec. 31, 2027) — Washoe County, "Lake Tahoe and tributaries" listing
sources.quotedText: Lake Tahoe and tributaries. Open year around except for closed areas. Daily: 5 game fish. Possession: 10 game fish. 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset

sources.title: CR 25-16 — Washoe County CLOSED WATERS (the only individually-closed Lake Tahoe tributaries are Third, Incline, and Wood creeks; First Creek is not listed)
sources.quotedText: CLOSED WATERS: Third Creek and its tributaries from Lake Tahoe to the Mt. Rose Highway No. 431. Incline Creek and its tributaries from Lake Tahoe to Ski Way and the Mt. Rose Highway No. 431. Wood Creek from Lake Tahoe to the Mt. Rose Highway No. 431. Lake Tahoe within a 200-yard radius of the mouths of Third, Incline and Wood creeks. A 500-yard radius from Sand Harbor boat ramp. Truckee River from Derby Dam downstream 1,000 feet.

sources.title: First Creek (waterway=stream) — OpenStreetMap, Incline Village / Crystal Bay area, north-shore Lake Tahoe
USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 859709, "First Creek", Stream, Washoe County, NV
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":859709,"gaz_name":"First Creek","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"NV","county_name":"Washoe"} — geometry multipoint; mouth point (Web Mercator -13357248.3222, 4757373.1225) converts to lon -119.99020, lat 39.24880, source point (Web Mercator -13357403.0456, 4760767.7775) converts to lon -119.99159, lat 39.27241. The file previously recorded -119.98593, 39.2617 (an unsourced estimate roughly midway between the two, ~1.5 km from the GNIS mouth point and ~1.3 km from the GNIS source point); corrected to the GNIS mouth coordinate.

sources.title: Apply & Buy — Nevada Fishing Licenses — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

parameters.note: First Creek is a north-shore Lake Tahoe tributary in Washoe County, Nevada — it flows south out of the Carson Range into Lake Tahoe near the Incline Village/Crystal Bay line, and all of its mapped course lies east of the CA/NV state line (long. -120.0), so it is governed by NDOW, not CDFW. The applicable Washoe County listing is 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' — open year around except for closed areas. First Creek is NOT among Washoe County's individually-closed Lake Tahoe tributaries (only Third, Incline, and Wood creeks are closed 'from Lake Tahoe to the Mt. Rose Highway'), so it is open all year. NOTE the state-line contrast: California-side Lake Tahoe tributaries are subject to a seasonal spawning closure under CCR T14 §7.50(b)(152) (open only Sat. before Memorial Day–Sep. 30, catch-and-release, artificial barbless), but that CDFW rule does not reach First Creek because it is in Nevada; Nevada keeps 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' open year-round except for the specifically closed creeks.

regulations.citation: NV CR 25-16 (Washoe County — Lake Tahoe and tributaries)
regulations.humanSummary: Open year-round (Nevada / Washoe County 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' listing; First Creek is a Lake Tahoe tributary and is not among the county's individually-closed creeks — Third, Incline, Wood).
regulations.verbatimText: Lake Tahoe and tributaries. Open year around except for closed areas.

parameters.counting_rule: Washoe County 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' limit: 5 game fish daily, 10 game fish in possession. 'Game fish' is the combined game-fish count; no species sub-limit is listed for this row in CR 25-16.

regulations.citation: NV CR 25-16 (Washoe County — Lake Tahoe and tributaries)
regulations.humanSummary: 5 game fish per day, 10 in possession (Nevada Washoe County 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' limit).
regulations.verbatimText: Lake Tahoe and tributaries. Daily: 5 game fish. Possession: 10 game fish.

parameters.note: The 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' row carries restricted hours (1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset) — narrower than Washoe County's general 'All waters' rule of any hour of the day or night.

regulations.citation: NV CR 25-16 (Washoe County — Lake Tahoe and tributaries)
regulations.humanSummary: Fishing permitted from 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset.
regulations.verbatimText: 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset

parameters.note: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers 12 and older. First Creek is a Nevada tributary (Washoe Co.), not the open water of Lake Tahoe; the Lake Tahoe CA/NV reciprocal-license arrangement is written for fishing anywhere ON the lake, so a Nevada license (not a California license alone) should be carried on the creek.

regulations.humanSummary: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers age 12 and older; none required under 12.
regulations.verbatimText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.
